articles.title: National rollout of a brief suicide prevention program for veterans shows high success rates
articles.summary: A recent study suggests that a brief suicide prevention program is being successfully delivered to at-risk veterans across the Veterans Health Administration. These findings offer insights that can help healthcare systems improve their suicide prevention strategies.
articles.aiSummary: A recent study suggests that a brief suicide prevention program is being successfully delivered to at-risk veterans across the Veterans Health Administration. These findings offer insights that can help healthcare systems improve their suicide prevention strategies.
articles.aiSummaryEn: A recent study suggests that a brief suicide prevention program is being successfully delivered to at-risk veterans across the Veterans Health Administration. These findings offer insights that can help healthcare systems improve their suicide prevention strategies.

articles.title: A diverse toy environment is linked to better infant communication skills
articles.summary: A home filled with a diverse array of toys may provide more than just entertainment. New research links household toy abundance to calmer infant temperaments, richer maternal teaching interactions, and stronger early communication skills.
articles.aiSummary: A home filled with a diverse array of toys may provide more than just entertainment. New research links household toy abundance to calmer infant temperaments, richer maternal teaching interactions, and stronger early communication skills.

articles.title: Reward Valuation in Vision Language Models: Causal Mechanisms Underlying Anhedonia
articles.summary: arXiv:2607.06626v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recent Vision-Language Models capture increasingly complex aspects of human cognition. Here we ask whether this alignment extends to reward valuation, which we assess in a mechanistic framework built on clinical tests that were developed to evaluate anhedonia and motivational deficits in major depressive disorder. In the brain, anhedonia is frequently linked to dysregulation in the Nucleus Accumbens (NAc) and the broader dopaminergic reward system. While neuroimaging has localized these deficits, establishing a causal link between NAc activity

articles.title: STST-JEPA: Shallow-Target Spatio-Temporal Joint Embedding Prediction Architecture For EEG Self-Supervised Learning
articles.summary: arXiv:2607.06629v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Brain age -- the age inferred from a physiological recording -- is an emerging biomarker whose deviation from chronological age tracks neurological and psychiatric burden, and EEG is an attractive substrate for it because it is cheap, portable, and temporally rich. Yet EEG brain-age models must contend with cross-site montage heterogeneity, small labelled cohorts, and dominant subject-level non-stationarity, and few EEG foundation models have been shown to deliver competitive age regression across the full pediatric-to-older-adult range in whic

articles.title: Single-Entity Spiking Neuron Models: Survey
articles.summary: arXiv:2607.07429v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: In this work, we reviewed different approaches in mathematical modeling of biologically plausible neural systems. Models are characterized and classified based on their common features and special use cases. In addition to spiking models, different types of discrete and continuous analogs are considered to accurately simulate biological processes, including membrane potential dynamics. The models under investigation include neurons and various components encountered in neural systems and affected the dynamics. The selection of specific approach

articles.title: Behavioral Scientist’s Summer Book List 2026
articles.summary: For your intellectual travels this summer, we’ve curated a baker’s dozen of behavioral science books newly published this year. Explore the mysteries of life and consciousness, glimpse the power of constraints and the games that define our lives, navigate life’s cliffs, and find the places that will make you who you are. — Heather Graci, Editor and Evan Nesterak, Editor-in-Chief Exploring life, death, and consciousness “Life begins, with a spark of sentience catalyzed by chemistry and chance, and then it ends, with death,” writes Maria Popova. “The great mystery is not the fable of what lies b

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…an Air Traffic Controller
articles.summary: Sequencing departures like a moving puzzle, scrambling when a jet takes off without clearance, and catching what radar misses with a glance out the window with Michael Rejent, an air traffic controller. articles.title: Chronic alcohol use alters Alzheimer’s brain circuits differently based on underlying pathology
articles.summary: New research reveals that chronic alcohol consumption rewires a key decision-making brain circuit in opposite ways, depending on whether the brain is burdened by Alzheimer's-related amyloid plaques or tau tangles.
articles.aiSummary: New research reveals that chronic alcohol consumption rewires a key decision-making brain circuit in opposite ways, depending on whether the brain is burdened by Alzheimer's-related amyloid plaques or tau tangles.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Flight Attendant
articles.summary: Hugging a mother through an anxiety attack, bidding for the best routes, and absorbing strangers' confessions at 35,000 feet with Linda Beall, a flight attendant. What does it mean when the cabin bells ring three or four times? And what makes a trip a “dog”? [buzzsprout episode='19322844' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... articles.title: How Can We Feel Loved If We Don’t Feel Known?
articles.summary: For Sonja, researching happiness means being asked over and over again, “What is the secret to happiness?” Sonja has always dreaded that question. It’s ridiculous, it’s reductive, and it’s restrictive. But mostly she has dreaded it because she has never had a solid, science-based answer that she could offer with any confidence. There is no secret to happiness. But there are huge contributors to happiness, and some of them are more powerful than others. Sonja resists being pinned down to just one. But if she were pressed to give an answer, she now has one. She found it when she met Harry, who s

articles.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's spreads through the brain
articles.summary: A common brain protein may be giving Alzheimer’s disease an unexpected way to spread, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one day be possible to slow the disease's relentless progression.
articles.aiSummary: A common brain protein may be giving Alzheimer’s disease an unexpected way to spread, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one day be possible to slow the disease's relentless progression.

By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one day be possible to slow the disease's relentless progression.

articles.title: Scientists say creatine may help fight depression
articles.summary: Creatine is best known as a muscle-building supplement, but scientists are now investigating whether it could also help treat depression by boosting the brain's energy supply. A new review examined five randomized clinical trials involving 238 participants and found mixed results. Two studies, both involving women with major depressive disorder, reported that adding creatine to standard treatment improved symptoms, while three others found no meaningful benefit.

articles.title: The political realignment of America: Education overtakes race as key ideological divider
articles.summary: Education is replacing race as the primary ideological divider in American politics, a new study finds. Less-educated Americans of all races are moving toward conservatism, while college graduates are becoming more liberal.
articles.aiSummary: Education is replacing race as the primary ideological divider in American politics, a new study finds. Less-educated Americans of all races are moving toward conservatism, while college graduates are becoming more liberal.
articles.aiSummaryEn: Education is replacing race as the primary ideological divider in American politics, a new study finds. Less-educated Americans of all races are moving toward conservatism, while college graduates are becoming more liberal.

articles.title: Men who consume pornography report lower sexual satisfaction than female viewers
articles.summary: A new meta-analysis of over 138,000 adults reveals a stark gender divide in how explicit media is consumed. While men view more pornography, they frequently report lower sexual function and satisfaction than female viewers.
articles.aiSummary: A new meta-analysis of over 138,000 adults reveals a stark gender divide in how explicit media is consumed. While men view more pornography, they frequently report lower sexual function and satisfaction than female viewers.
articles.aiSummaryEn: A new meta-analysis of over 138,000 adults reveals a stark gender divide in how explicit media is consumed. While men view more pornography, they frequently report lower sexual function and satisfaction than female viewers.

articles.title: Scientists reveal a simple feedback tweak that could improve human-machine interface control
articles.summary: Learning to control a robotic limb or recover movement after a stroke is incredibly difficult. But a new study reveals that providing real-time, instantaneous feedback about task success can dramatically boost motor control when sensory information is missing.
articles.aiSummary: Learning to control a robotic limb or recover movement after a stroke is incredibly difficult. But a new study reveals that providing real-time, instantaneous feedback about task success can dramatically boost motor control when sensory information is missing.
articles.aiSummaryEn: Learning to control a robotic limb or recover movement after a stroke is incredibly difficult. But a new study reveals that providing real-time, instantaneous feedback about task success can dramatically boost motor control when sensory information is missing.

articles.title: Scientists discover why some brains resist Alzheimer's
articles.summary: Some brains appear to fight back against Alzheimer's by helping immature brain cells survive damage instead of succumbing to it. Understanding this natural resilience could point researchers toward entirely new ways to protect memory and slow dementia.
articles.aiSummary: Some brains appear to fight back against Alzheimer's by helping immature brain cells survive damage instead of succumbing to it. Understanding this natural resilience could point researchers toward entirely new ways to protect memory and slow dementia.
articles.aiSummaryEn: Some brains appear to fight back against Alzheimer's by helping immature brain cells survive damage instead of succumbing to it. Understanding this natural resilience could point researchers toward entirely new ways to protect memory and slow dementia.

articles.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's kills brain cells
articles.summary: Researchers have identified a previously overlooked mechanism of brain cell death that appears to play a major role in Alzheimer's disease and frontotemporal dementia. The finding could lead to new treatments aimed at slowing neuron loss by interrupting the process before cells are destroyed.
articles.aiSummary: Researchers have identified a previously overlooked mechanism of brain cell death that appears to play a major role in Alzheimer's disease and frontotemporal dementia. The finding could lead to new treatments aimed at slowing neuron loss by interrupting the process before cells are destroyed.

articles.title: American ginseng extract improves memory and clearing of cellular waste in aging rats
articles.summary: A botanical extract derived from American ginseng can boost the brain's cellular waste disposal system, protecting synapses from age-related damage. In a recent rat study, the supplement successfully reversed cognitive decline and improved learning and memory.
articles.aiSummary: A botanical extract derived from American ginseng can boost the brain's cellular waste disposal system, protecting synapses from age-related damage. In a recent rat study, the supplement successfully reversed cognitive decline and improved learning and memory.
articles.aiSummaryEn: A botanical extract derived from American ginseng can boost the brain's cellular waste disposal system, protecting synapses from age-related damage. In a recent rat study, the supplement successfully reversed cognitive decline and improved learning and memory.

articles.title: New psychology study challenges a major assumption about why we bond with our friends
articles.summary: We often assume that birds of a feather flock together. However, new psychological research suggests that personality similarity doesn't actually predict friendship satisfaction. Instead, how you perceive your friends' positive traits plays a much bigger role.
articles.aiSummary: We often assume that birds of a feather flock together. However, new psychological research suggests that personality similarity doesn't actually predict friendship satisfaction. Instead, how you perceive your friends' positive traits plays a much bigger role.

articles.title: AgentLens: Production-Assessed Trajectory Reviews for Coding Agent Evaluation
articles.summary: arXiv:2607.06624v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present AgentLens, a production-assessed benchmark for interactive code agents. Most code-agent benchmarks reduce a run to a single bit -- did the task pass? -- but the people who actually use these agents experience the entire trajectory: how the agent follows instructions, uses its tools, verifies its own work, recovers from mistakes, and talks to them along the way. AgentLens evaluates that whole trajectory. It pairs formal verification, where an objective check exists, with LLM-written trajectory reviews and side-by-side comparisons, so th
